data.title: Gate.io MemeBox Announces Strategic Partnership with BNB Chain
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, recently, Gate.io MemeBox officially announced a strategic cooperation with BNB Chain, aiming to promote the further development of the Meme coin market through a series of activities.
This cooperation marks Gate.io's diversified layout in the field of Meme coin trading. In the future, it will provide a series of new opportunities for BNB Chain's Meme coin projects, especially the top ten projects on the BNB Chain Meme Liquidity Support Program. They will have the opportunity to be listed in the Gate.io Innovation Zone, and provide multi-faceted exposure opportunities and resource support for the listed projects. This move will provide real growth for the project and help it occupy a place in the rapidly developing market.
It is reported that the Gate.io Innovation Zone is committed to integrating the advantages of spot trading and on-chain trading, and innovatively lowering the technical threshold for users to participate in on-chain projects. Users do not need to operate Web3 wallets or manage private keys. They can directly participate in hot projects on the chain with a Gate.io account, thereby accurately capturing early investment opportunities.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 650 USDT with a 2.80% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 24, 2025, 02:24 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 650 USDT and is now trading at 649.809998 USDT, with a narrowed 2.80% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Crypto Exchange eXch Denies Involvement in Bybit Hack Money Laundering
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, crypto exchange eXch has refuted allegations of laundering money for North Korea’s Lazarus Group following a significant hack on Bybit, which resulted in a $1.4 billion loss on February 21, 2025. In a statement released on February 23 via the Bitcointalk forum, eXch asserted that it is not involved in laundering funds for Lazarus or the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK). The exchange emphasized that its operations remain unaffected by the Bybit hack and assured that all funds are secure.

The exchange acknowledged processing a minor portion of the hacked funds but clarified that this was an isolated incident. eXch stated that the funds in question entered their address, 0xf1da173228fcf015f43f3ea15abbb51f0d8f1123, and that any fees generated from this transaction would be donated for public benefit. The exchange further clarified that no other addresses on the Ethereum blockchain, aside from deposit addresses interacting with this specific address, are linked to eXch.

The statement from eXch appears to be a response to social media allegations, including claims from onchain investigator ZachXBT, who accused eXch of laundering $35 million of the stolen funds. ZachXBT alleged that eXch inadvertently sent 34 Ether (ETH), valued at $96,000, to a hot wallet of another exchange. Additional accusations came from blockchain analysts and the security firm SlowMist, who claimed that eXch received Ether from wallets associated with the Bybit hack. Nick Bax, a member of the Security Alliance, estimated that eXch processed approximately $30 million in volume for DPRK on the day of the hack.

In response to the hack, Bybit has managed to freeze over $42 million of the stolen funds through coordinated efforts. However, Bybit has reportedly faced resistance from eXch in freezing the remaining funds. eXch accused Bybit of previously freezing its users’ funds and failing to communicate effectively to resolve these issues. Bybit CEO Ben Zhou expressed hope that eXch would reconsider its stance and assist in blocking the outflow of funds, emphasizing the importance of a unified industry approach against hackers.

data.title: Singapore-Based Bgin Blockchain Seeks $50 Million Through U.S. IPO
data.description: According to PANews, Singapore-based cryptocurrency mining equipment manufacturer Bgin Blockchain Limited has filed for a public listing in the United States, aiming to raise $50 million. The company submitted documents to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on February 21, indicating plans to issue approximately 59.54 million Class A ordinary shares and 15.69 million Class B ordinary shares. Bgin has also applied to list its Class A shares on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol 'BGIN.' While the filing did not disclose the offering price, investment advisory firm Renaissance Capital reported on February 21 that Bgin's initial public offering (IPO) could raise up to $50 million.

data.title: Bybit Hacker Transfers Assets to New Address
data.description: According to Foresight News, Beosin monitoring has reported that a new address linked to the Bybit hacker transferred assets 40 minutes ago. Earlier today at 4:00 UTC+8, the old address beginning with 0xfc92 completed an asset transfer, sending tokens to the new address.

data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Perhaps affected by pump.fun's test of the Swap module, RAY fell below 3.5 USDT and is now trading at 3.47 USDT, with a 24-hour drop of 18.94%.

data.title: Roam launches interactive creation bounty program with a total prize pool of 100,000 ROAM
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Roam officially launched the community bounty program, with a total prize pool of 100,000 ROAM tokens, encouraging users to participate and promote community development. The event will be held from February 22 to March 21.
The program is divided into four major sections: content creation (40,000 ROAM), social media interaction (20,000 ROAM), community participation (20,000 ROAM) and outstanding performance (20,000 ROAM). Content creators can participate through articles, videos or infographics, and selected works will be rewarded each week. Social media interactors have the opportunity to win rewards by posting, liking, forwarding, commenting or participating in Twitter Space on X. Community participation rewards the top 10 active users in Telegram and Discord. The outstanding performance section flexibly distributes rewards to high-quality content creators or loyal users.
The way to participate is simple: register for the Roam App and follow the task requirements. The list of winners will be announced every Friday, and the rewards will be distributed within 7 days.
Currently, Roam has 2.3 million registered users and nearly 2 million self-built WiFi nodes, covering more than 190 countries and regions. According to the Messari 2024 report, Roam ranks fourth among the "DePIN projects with more than one million active nodes worldwide" and ranks first in the hardware node list. This bounty program will further enhance community vitality and demonstrate Roam's strong growth potential.
